channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners after Hamas frees 3 Israeli hostages
item.description: Israel has freed 90 Palestinian prisoners and detainees. The release came early Monday, more than seven hours after three Israeli hostages released from Hamas captivity in Gaza returned to Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Couple accused of stealing $315K from guest at Ritz-Carlton: Prosecutors
item.description: Police said the victim reported his hotel room had been ransacked and that $315,000 in cash had been stolen from his suitcase.
item.dc:creator: Kevin S. Held, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok restores service for US users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: TikTok restored service to users in the United States on Sunday just hours after the popular video-sharing platform went dark in response to a federal ban, which President-elect Donald Trump said he would try to pause by executive order on his first day in office.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigrants in Chicago and other US cities brace for expected Trump deportation arrests
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Rev. Homero Sanchez said he didn't realize the depth of fear in the Chicago immigrant community he serves until someone asked him to handle the sale of their family's home and other finances if they are picked up this week when President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's inauguration eve rally
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rallied with supporters in Washington, D.C., on the eve of his second inauguration, taking a victory lap and teasing an expected barrage of activity on his first day in office. Here are five takeaways from Trump’s rally at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump plans barrage of Day 1 executive actions on border, energy, federal workforce
item.description: President-elect Trump is planning a barrage of executive actions on energy, the border and immigration enforcement on his first day in office, four sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, KREX 5

item.title: More than 100 Hawaii homeless sent back to mainland families
item.description: "To date, we have sent 137 people from the mainland who are houseless back to their families on the mainland, saving taxpayer costs," John Mizuno said.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Biden pardons the late Black nationalist Marcus Garvey as well as 4 others and commutes 2 sentences
item.description: President Joe Biden on Sunday posthumously pardoned Black nationalist Marcus Garvey, who influenced Malcolm X and other civil rights leaders and was convicted of mail fraud in the 1920s.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok says it's in the process of restoring service to US users
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States were no longer able to watch or post videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular video-sharing app took effect Sunday amid questions over how long the blackout might last.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s inauguration performers include A-list country artists, rappers, bands
item.description: Donald Trump is bringing out the star power for his inauguration performances. The Trump-Vance Inaugural Committee announced the lineup for the 60th Presidential Inauguration Weekend earlier this week, which, according to the committee, features “some of our nation’s most iconic musicians.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Why are lone homes left standing after the fires? It's not entirely luck
item.description: Emails and videos of burned buildings in Los Angeles next to those left standing have been flying back and forth among architects, builders and fire safety specialists around the world.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY and INGRID LOBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that’s probably because it has, at least if you’re measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: David Hamilton, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missouri lawmakers are going after voter-approved abortion rights. Voters will likely reelect them
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Voters in Missouri last election approved a constitutional amendment that promised to undo the state's near-total abortion ban.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Travis Kelce makes NFL history in first game of three-peat quest
item.description: Once again, Travis Kelce has broken a record at Arrowhead Stadium after catching a pass from Patrick Mahomes.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eggs are about to get even more expensive, expert says. Here's how much
item.description: Eggs are nearly 40% more expensive now than they were a year ago, the Labor Department said this week. It's about to get even worse, one expert says.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ will give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he will likely give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Private college students in ‘To Catch a Predator’ TikTok trend targeting Army soldier plead not guilty
item.description: Six Assumption University students face charges after allegedly luring an Army soldier, 22, to their campus to confront him and film the encounter, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KRON4

item.title: US to hit 2 million annual new cancer cases for the first time
item.description: The number of cancer cases is expected to cross the two million mark in 2024 -- the first time ever -- according American Cancer Society estimates.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president. He did that for George H.W. Bush, his successor and vice president of eight years.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Guard troops will wear a special patch at Trump's inauguration to make it clear they aren't police
item.description: National Guard leaders have authorized the use of a special shoulder patch with the Guard motto “Always Ready, Always There," to ensure that people coming to the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump on Monday will know who is who.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: State Farm cancels Super Bowl ad over California wildfires
item.description: Insurance giant State Farm has canceled a Super Bowl ad that was set to air during the big game in February, in part, it said, over the raging wildfires in California.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A battery plant fire in California started during a boom for energy storage
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A fire at one of the world’s largest battery plants in Northern California contained tens of thousands of lithium batteries that store power from renewable energy and have become a growing electricity source.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 6 people injured after dump truck crashes into New Jersey Target
item.description: A dump truck crashed into a Target in New Jersey Friday, opening up a large hole in the side of the building and leaving several people injured.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Tunnel used to smuggle migrants, weapons located near El Paso
item.description: A tunnel that has been used for a year is used to smuggle migrants, weapons and cash across the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Engineer Tedesco brothers use retrofitted RV to track UAPs
item.description: Two brothers have retrofitted an RV into a mobile lab to collect data about UAPs and other mysterious flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, Fox 5

item.title: How to prepare for a TikTok ban, including how to save your content
item.description: TikTok has cemented itself as a quintessential entertainment app, offering everything from funny skits and makeup tutorials to social commentary and news. Without a sale to an approved buyer, the platform is expected to vanish from U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu says deal to release hostages held in Gaza has been reached after last minute snags
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Friday a deal to return hostages held in the Gaza Strip has been reached, after his office said earlier there were last minute snags in finalizing a ceasefire that would pause 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WFLA, NBC
